school.name: The Schoolhouse
school.description: Each teacher at the schoolhouse leads a different class in a different room. Work out who teaches what, and where.
clues: Of Boyd and the class in the Pavilion, whoever has the higher grade has the lower period.
Period 1 goes with exactly two of Ames, Annex, and 77%.
Either Pavilion goes with Ellis or Period 5 goes with Hale (but not both) if and only if either 82% goes with Studio or Period 4 does not go with Ellis (but not both).
Period 2 goes with exactly two of Ellis, Library, and 82%.
The class with 77%'s period is higher than both Ellis' period and the class in the Cloister's period.
Cloister, 92%, and Period 3 go with Ames, Ellis, and Hale, in some order.
Hale goes with at least two of Cloister, 97%, and Period 5.

chess.name: The Chess Club
chess.description: Regulars at the chess club each swear by a different opening, a favourite tactic, and a prized set. Work out who plays what.
clues: Of Boris and the Pirc player, whoever has the higher rating has the lower placing.
5th goes with exactly two of Anatoly, Benoni, and 1689.
Either Pirc goes with Magnus or 1st goes with Nadia (but not both) if and only if either 1789 goes with Slav or 2nd does not go with Magnus (but not both).
4th goes with exactly two of Magnus, London, and 1789.
The 1689-rated player's placing is higher than both Magnus' placing and the French player's placing.
French, 1989, and 3rd go with Anatoly, Magnus, and Nadia, in some order.
Nadia goes with at least two of French, 2089, and 1st.
